name: Lower Saxony (Germany)

colors.symbolism: from the German national flag; also the field of the Saxon coat of arms

description: A horizontal black-red-gold tricolour (the German national colours) defaced with the shield of Lower Saxony in the centre: a leaping white horse on a red field.
history: Adopted in 1952 with the formation of the West German state of Lower Saxony from the former Kingdom of Hanover, Brunswick, Oldenburg, and Schaumburg-Lippe. It is one of several German Land flags that use the federal tricolour as its base, defacing it with the state arms.

features.symbolism: The German national tricolour, charged with the state arms, defacing the federal flag to identify Lower Saxony.

features.role: shield bearing the white Saxon horse (Sachsenross)
features.symbolism: The leaping white horse on a red field is the historic emblem of Saxony, dating back to medieval Welf and Saxon heraldry; it identifies the state of Lower Saxony, which inherited the heraldic legacy of the duchies of Saxony, Brunswick-Lüneburg, and Oldenburg.
